description_long: This is an electric guitar with a striking, angular body shape reminiscent of the Gibson Explorer, finished in a glossy black color. The body features two control knobs and a metallic bridge, with a pickguard that contrasts against the dark body. The neck is made of a lighter wood, likely maple, and is fitted with six tuning pegs on a headstock that is slightly angled and elongated. The fretboard appears to be rosewood or a similar dark wood, with visible frets and dot inlays. The guitar is equipped with two humbucker pickups, providing a powerful and versatile sound. The overall design is modern and aggressive, suitable for rock or metal genres and urban street performances.
description: A glossy black, angular electric guitar with a light wood neck, two humbucker pickups, and metallic hardware, designed for modern music styles.
description_view_0: A side profile showing the thinness of the guitar body and the protruding headstock.
description_view_1: A full view of the front, displaying the black angular body, metallic bridge, two control knobs, and the light wood neck with six tuning pegs.
description_view_2: A side profile from the opposite side, highlighting the neck and headstock alignment with the body.
description_view_3: A rear view showing the back of the black body and the full length of the neck and headstock.
attribute_headstock_design: Angled, elongated headstock with six inline tuning pegs and no visible logo
